question.text: Under the PMLA framework, who has the power to call for records from any reporting entity and additional information for the purposes of the Act?

answer.text: The Director (FIU-IND) as per Section 12A of PMLA

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 12A of PMLA states that the Director may call for from any reporting entity any of the records referred to in section 12, section 12AA and any additional information as he considers necessary for the purposes of this Act. **The Trap:** Option A (RBI) is tempting because RBI regulates banks which are also reporting entities, but under PMLA specifically, it is the Director (FIU-IND) who has this power. **Cross-Exam Context:** Powers of the Director under PMLA are tested in NISM Series III-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** All information sought by the Director shall be kept confidential — the reporting entity must furnish information within the time and manner specified by the Director.
